task.name: Interactive project discovery — BMAD-style collaborative session
task.objective: Guide the user through collaborative project discovery. Detect language/framework, produce product brief, and collect domain model inputs. This is the ONLY interactive phase — all subsequent planning and implementation is autonomous.

input.note: No $ARGUMENTS — discovery starts with the project directory as implicit input. The working directory IS the project.

output.primary: docs/architecture/product-brief.md — product brief with 5 required sections
output.secondary: Language and framework detection stored as session state (LANG, FRAMEWORK, TEMPLATE_DIR) for subsequent steps

requirements.note: No MCP server required. Discovery is a pure conversational skill using filesystem access (Read, Bash) for scanning and Write for saving documents.

glossary.term: Detected primary language slug (e.g. "php", "go", "python", "node"). Set in Step 1.
File path that triggered language detection (e.g. "composer.json"). Set in Step 1.

Path to language-specific templates. Resolved in Step 1 via two-tier lookup: try .tmux-cli/templates/<LANG>-<FRAMEWORK>/ first (normalized to lowercase-kebab), then .tmux-cli/templates/<LANG>/, then fall back to BASE_TEMPLATE_DIR.
Path to universal templates: .tmux-cli/templates/_base/. Always available.

llm.mandate: NEVER generate or modify source code — discovery produces documents only
NEVER skip user confirmation of language detection — Step 1 blocks until confirmed
NEVER use AskUserQuestion — all interaction via text output + waiting for user messages (tmux window compatibility)
ALWAYS present findings conversationally, then invite refinement — collaborative, not interrogative
ALWAYS validate product brief has all 5 sections non-empty (D-04) before saving
ALWAYS create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist before writing output files

step.title: Read project context and templates
step.action: Check if AGENTS.md exists in the project root. If it does, Read it and internalize project conventions, architecture, and constraints.
If AGENTS.md does not exist, check for CLAUDE.md instead. Read whichever is found first.
If neither exists, proceed without project context.

Read the base discovery template at .tmux-cli/templates/_base/discovery.md. This defines the question structure for Steps 1 and 2. Internalize its sections but do NOT dump it to the user — use it as a reference for conversational flow.
step.rule: Project context and template content MUST inform your conversational style, question framing, and validation throughout all subsequent steps.

step.title: Language detection
step.action: Scan project root for language indicator files:

pattern.framework-hint: require keys containing symfony/*, laravel/*, etc.
dependencies containing react, next, express, etc.
require lines containing gin, echo, fiber, etc.
dependencies containing actix, rocket, axum, etc.
dependencies containing django, flask, fastapi, etc.
lines containing django, flask, fastapi, etc.
gem lines containing rails, sinatra, etc.

branch.action: Read the manifest file. Extract LANG and FRAMEWORK from its contents.
Set DETECTED_SOURCE to the manifest filename.
branch.template:
I scanned the project and found **{{DETECTED_SOURCE}}**. Based on its contents, this looks like a **{{FRAMEWORK}}** project using **{{LANG}}**.
[cite specific evidence — e.g. "composer.json lists symfony/framework-bundle in require"]
Does this look right, or should I adjust?

branch.condition: no manifest found (empty or non-code project)
branch.template:
I didn't find any language indicator files (no package.json, composer.json, go.mod, etc.). This looks like a fresh project.
What programming language and framework will this project use?
branch.action: Wait for user response. Set LANG and FRAMEWORK from their answer.

step.action: On confirmation: normalize LANG and FRAMEWORK to lowercase-kebab (e.g. "Ruby on Rails" → LANG=ruby, FRAMEWORK=rails). Resolve TEMPLATE_DIR via two-tier lookup: 1. Check .tmux-cli/templates/{{LANG}}-{{FRAMEWORK}}/ — if it exists, use it. 2. Else check .tmux-cli/templates/{{LANG}}/ — if it exists, use it. 3. Else fall back to .tmux-cli/templates/_base/ (BASE_TEMPLATE_DIR).
Read TEMPLATE_DIR/discovery.md (if present) for language-specific follow-ups in later steps. If only _base/ was resolved, note that only base templates apply.
step.rule: Do NOT proceed to Step 2 until language is confirmed by the user.

step.title: Product brief
step.action: Open with context-setting introduction:
step.template: Now let's define the product. I'll ask about five areas — feel free to be brief or detailed, we can refine as we go.
step.action: Ask each question one at a time, conversationally. Do not dump all five as a numbered list. Reference the question intent from _base/discovery.md section 2 but phrase naturally:

questions.q: What problem does this project solve? What pain point or gap exists today?
Who are the target users or personas? Who will use this and what are their roles?
What is the MVP scope — the minimum set of features for initial release?
What is explicitly out of scope for the MVP? What will you intentionally NOT build yet?
How will you know the MVP is working? What are the success metrics?

step.rule: Ask ONE question, wait for answer, then ask the next. Do not batch.
step.action: After all 5 answers collected, compose docs/architecture/product-brief.md:
Validate: check that all 5 sections are present and non-empty.

check.action: Ask a targeted follow-up for that specific section:
check.template: The **{{section_name}}** section is still empty. Could you share even a brief answer? For example: {{contextual hint based on the question}}.
check.action: Wait for response and update that section.

step.action: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist (mkdir -p).
Present summary back to user before saving:
On confirmation, write the file.
step.rule: Do NOT save product-brief.md until user confirms the summary.
D-04 gate: product brief must contain Problem, Target Users, MVP Scope, Out of Scope, Success Metrics — all non-empty.

step.title: Domain Discovery
step.action: Read _base/discovery.md sections 3-5 and 7-8 to inform conversational question flow. Do NOT dump template content to the user — use it as reference for natural follow-up questions.

substep.title: Bounded Context Identification
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 3 questions conversationally. Help the user identify the major domain areas (bounded contexts) in their project. For each BC, collect a short name and description.
Present collected BCs as a summary table and ask the user to confirm or adjust.

check.action: Display error: "At least one bounded context is required to proceed. Please describe the main domain area of your project."
Loop back to the BC identification question. Do NOT proceed to substep 3.2 or any downstream step with zero BCs. (D-28)

substep.action: Count confirmed BCs. If exactly one, set is_single_bc=true. Note this for later steps — context map and ACL questions will be skipped. (D-27)

For each BC, confirm no entity is shared across BC boundaries (D-07). Ask: "Are there any entities that live in more than one bounded context? If so, which ones, and how should they be separated?" Cross-BC references must use foreign ID value objects only.

action.condition: LANG is php and FRAMEWORK is symfony

substep.action: After base questions, ask php-symfony/discovery.md section 3a questions: map each BC to Symfony bundles, identify third-party bundles from config/bundles.php, and check for custom bundles under src/.

substep.title: Aggregates and Domain Events
substep.action: For each bounded context identified in 3.1, ask _base/discovery.md section 4 questions conversationally.

For each BC, identify aggregate roots — the main entity controlling a cluster of related objects. Capture: aggregate name, root entity, value objects, and invariants (business rules that must always hold). Every BC must have at least one aggregate root. (D-06)
For each aggregate, identify domain events — facts about things that happened. Capture: event name and trigger description. Do not skip domain events discovery. (D-08)

action.condition: LANG is php and FRAMEWORK is symfony

substep.action: After base questions, ask php-symfony/discovery.md section 4a questions: Doctrine mapping strategy (attributes/annotations/XML/YAML), custom DBAL types for value objects, Doctrine Migrations config, and entity manager count (read/write split, separate managers per BC).

substep.title: Domain Services
substep.action: For each bounded context, ask _base/discovery.md section 5 questions conversationally.
Identify operations that span multiple aggregates WITHIN the same BC but don't naturally belong to any one aggregate. These are domain services. (D-22)
For each domain service, capture: service name, which aggregates it spans, and operation description.
If no cross-aggregate operations exist in a BC, record "none" for that BC and move on.

substep.title: Context Mapping
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 7 questions conversationally. This substep is skipped entirely for single-BC projects. (D-27)

For each pair of bounded contexts that communicate, capture: upstream BC, downstream BC, relationship type (Conformist, Customer-Supplier, Published Language, Shared Kernel, Anti-Corruption Layer, Open Host Service), and ACL interface name where applicable. (D-21)
Ask about isolated BCs — any bounded contexts that should have no direct communication with others.

substep.title: Share Namespace
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 8 questions conversationally.

Identify shared value objects, data types, and enums used across multiple bounded contexts (e.g., Money, DateRange, Address, Email, Status enums). These belong in a shared namespace outside any single BC.
For each shared type, confirm with the user whether it should be a single shared definition or duplicated per BC.

output.file: Per-BC aggregate roots with entities, value objects, invariants, domain events with triggers, and domain services, share namespace. Uses _base/domain-model.md template as skeleton.
BC inventory with relationships, ACL boundaries, share namespace, and dependency rules. Uses _base/bounded-contexts.md template as skeleton.
Translation strategies, boundary error handling, event choreography, and consistency patterns between BCs. Uses _base/context-mapping.md template as skeleton. Not generated for single-BC projects.

step.rule: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist before writing output files.
Present a summary of the domain model to the user and ask for confirmation before saving files.

step.title: Architecture Decisions
step.action: Read _base/discovery.md sections 6 and 12 to inform conversational question flow. Do NOT dump template content to the user.

substep.title: Core Infrastructure Decisions
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 6 questions conversationally. Cover key architecture decisions:

decisions.decision: Multi-database setup, read replicas, separate DB per BC
Message bus (synchronous, async with Messenger, RabbitMQ)

Run target — DETECT first, then confirm (never silently default to local on a host with no PHP). STEP 1, probe the host with these commands and record results: `command -v php`, `php -v` (compare the version to composer.json require.php when present), `command -v composer`, `command -v docker`, `docker compose version`. STEP 2, form a RECOMMENDATION: (a) a local PHP toolchain present AND satisfying the project's PHP constraint → local is viable; (b) local PHP ABSENT or version-mismatched while Docker + Compose are present → RECOMMEND docker (a containerized runtime avoids the missing-/wrong-local-PHP failure that otherwise only surfaces mid-build at Gate 0 / scaffold); (c) neither PHP nor Docker present → flag both missing and recommend installing Docker. STEP 3, present the detection summary and recommendation and ask the user to confirm or override: "Detected: <php/composer/docker findings>. Recommended RUN_TARGET = <docker|local> because <reason>. Use this, or override?" Capture the discrete value RUN_TARGET = docker | local from their confirmation — detection drives the DEFAULT, the user still decides. This drives Gate 0 (goal-001) prerequisite checks and the generation runtime model (task-plan-generate Step 0c CMD_PREFIX). If docker, also ask whether the database/services run as compose services (default) or are external. If docker AND the DB/services run as compose services, ALSO ask the published host:container port mappings the stack exposes for each service tests/host must reach (e.g. web 8080→80, db 5432→5432, redis 6379→6379). Capture a discrete PUBLISHED_PORTS list — service, host port, container port, purpose — distinct from the DB connection host/port used only for Gate-0 connectivity. Persist into test-environment.md (Published Ports block) and the Goal-0 AGENTS.md; record it ONLY there, never as an ADR.

substep.action: For each decision, capture: context (why this decision matters), alternatives considered with pros/cons, and the chosen rationale.
Ask about any open or unresolved architecture decisions that need further investigation.

action.condition: LANG is php and FRAMEWORK is symfony

substep.action: After base questions, ask php-symfony/discovery.md section 6a questions: Deptrac layer enforcement configuration, CQRS bus separation strategy (single bus, separate command/query/event buses), and domain event dispatch strategy (after flush, before flush, manual, Doctrine lifecycle events).

substep.title: Cross-Cutting Patterns
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 12 questions conversationally. Cover cross-cutting concerns that affect multiple BCs:

concerns.concern: Pagination strategy — cursor-based, offset-limit, or page-number
Soft-delete policy — which entities support soft-delete vs hard-delete
Timezone handling — UTC storage with user-local display, single timezone, etc.
Internationalisation and locale requirements — which languages, what content is translated
Error response format — RFC 7807 Problem Details, custom envelope, HTTP status only
Middleware and request/response filters — CORS, rate limiting, request logging, request ID

substep.action: For each cross-cutting concern, capture the chosen strategy and rationale.

substep.title: ADR Generation
substep.action: For each decision captured in substeps 4.1 and 4.2, generate one Architecture Decision Record (ADR) file using _base/adr.md template as skeleton.
Number ADRs sequentially starting at 001. Derive the filename slug from the decision topic (e.g., adr-001-database.md, adr-002-authentication.md, adr-003-caching.md).

Each ADR must contain: status (Accepted/Proposed), date, bounded context (which BC this decision primarily affects — use "Cross-Cutting" for decisions spanning multiple BCs), related ADRs (links to other ADRs this decision depends on or supersedes — use "None" if standalone), context (why the decision is needed), decision drivers (forces at play), the decision itself, consequences (positive, negative, and neutral), and alternatives considered with pros, cons, and rejection reason. These fields match the _base/adr.md template header and body sections. (D-09, D-10)

Note: language/framework-specific decisions from 4.1 (e.g., Deptrac layer enforcement, CQRS bus separation, event dispatch strategy for PHP/Symfony) also produce ADRs — they are included in "each decision captured in substeps 4.1 and 4.2" and must not be skipped.
substep.rule: EXCEPTION: the run-target decision (topic="run-target") is recorded ONLY in docs/architecture/test-environment.md (Step 7.3), NEVER as an ADR. Reason: task-plan-generate.xml scans ADRs for the keyword "docker" to decide whether to generate the production deployment goal (step 3.26); a run-target ADR containing "docker" would falsely trigger that deployment goal for a project the user only wants to RUN in a container. The free-form deployment decision/ADR stays the sole trigger for step 3.26 — the two questions are independent. The published host:container port mappings captured alongside run-target (PUBLISHED_PORTS) are LIKEWISE recorded ONLY in test-environment.md, NEVER as an ADR — they introduce no new ADR topic; test-environment.md is their sole sink.
substep.action: Create docs/architecture/adrs/ directory if it does not exist.
Present a summary of all ADRs to the user with decision topics and chosen options before saving files.

output.file: One ADR file per architecture decision, numbered sequentially. Uses _base/adr.md template as skeleton. Minimum set typically includes database, authentication, caching, API style — actual count depends on user decisions.

step.rule: ADR numbering is sequential and gap-free — adr-001, adr-002, adr-003, etc.
Present all generated ADRs for user review before saving to disk.

step.title: API Endpoint Inventory
step.action: Read _base/discovery.md section 9 to inform conversational question flow. Do NOT dump template content to the user — use it as reference for natural follow-up questions.

substep.title: Per-BC Endpoint Collection
substep.action: For each bounded context identified in Step 3.1, collect API endpoints conversationally. Present each BC in turn:
substep.template:
Let's map out the API endpoints for **{{bc_name}}**. What actions does this bounded context expose via API?
For each endpoint, I need: HTTP method, path, a short description, and whether authentication is required.
substep.action: Wait for user response. Collect endpoints into a per-BC table:
After collecting endpoints for one BC, present the table back and confirm before moving to the next BC.
substep.rule: Ask ONE BC at a time. Do not batch all BCs into a single question.

substep.title: Batch & Webhook Endpoints
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 9 questions 2-3 conversationally:
substep.template: Are there any batch or bulk endpoints needed? For example, endpoints that accept multiple items in a single request (bulk create, bulk update, bulk delete).
substep.action: Wait for response. Then ask about webhooks:
substep.template: Are there any webhook or callback endpoints? For example, endpoints that receive notifications from external services (payment confirmations, delivery status updates).
substep.action: Wait for response. Add any batch or webhook endpoints to the endpoint tables from substep 5.1.

substep.title: API Conventions
substep.action: Collect API-wide conventions that apply across all endpoints. Ask conversationally, one at a time:

q.template: What error response format does the API use? For example: RFC 7807 Problem Details, a custom envelope format, or just HTTP status codes?
What pagination strategy do list endpoints use? For example: cursor-based, offset-limit, or page-number?
How is the API versioned? For example: URL prefix (/v1/...), header-based, or no versioning?
Is there any rate limiting? If so, what strategy — per-user, per-IP, per-endpoint?

substep.rule: Ask ONE question, wait for answer, then ask the next. Do not batch.

output.file: Per-BC endpoint inventory tables, batch/webhook endpoints, and API conventions. Uses _base/api-endpoints.md template as skeleton.

step.rule: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist before writing output files.
Present a complete summary of all endpoints across all BCs to the user and ask for confirmation before saving to docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md.

step.title: Cross-Cutting Concerns
step.action: Read _base/discovery.md sections 10-11 and 13 to inform conversational question flow. Do NOT dump template content to the user — use it as reference for natural follow-up questions.

substep.title: Frontend Presence Detection
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 13 questions conversationally. Determine whether a frontend exists and what type.
Present options conversationally:
substep.template: Does this project have a frontend? If so, what kind? - Server-rendered (SSR / templates) - Single-page application (SPA) - Mobile app (native or cross-platform) - API-only (no frontend) - Something else?
substep.action: Wait for user response. Set frontend_type from their answer.

branch.action: Record frontend_type = "API-only" and skip remaining frontend questions (framework, protocol, shared contracts).
Ask follow-up questions one at a time:

questions.q: What frontend framework or technology is used?
How does the frontend communicate with the backend? (REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, etc.)
Are there any shared types or contracts between frontend and backend? (e.g., OpenAPI spec, generated types, shared schema)

branch.rule: Ask ONE question, wait for answer, then ask the next.

substep.title: Auth Flows
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 10 questions conversationally. This substep maps concrete auth flows — the auth METHOD was already decided in Step 4.1 ADR.
First, confirm the auth method chosen in Step 4.1:
substep.template: In the architecture decisions we settled on {{auth_method}} for authentication. Now let's map out the specific flows. For each of these, what steps are involved?
substep.action: Collect a flow table covering all 5 flow types:

flows.flow: Token refresh or session renewal steps

substep.rule: Ask about flows conversationally, not as a rigid form. Group related flows if the user prefers.
substep.action: Ask about user roles and permission levels:
substep.template: Are there different user roles or permission levels? If so, what are they?
substep.action: Wait for response.
Ask about permission enforcement:
substep.template: How should permissions be enforced? For example: middleware-based, per-handler checks, policy/voter objects, or attribute-based?
substep.action: Wait for response.

action.condition: LANG is php and FRAMEWORK is symfony

substep.action: After base auth questions, ask Symfony Security-specific questions from php-symfony/discovery.md section 10a: - What firewalls are configured in security.yaml? (firewall name, URL pattern, authenticator) - Are custom voters used for authorization decisions? - What access decision strategy is configured? (affirmative, consensus, unanimous)

substep.title: Event Listeners
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 11 questions conversationally. This substep covers system-level events BEYOND domain events from Step 3.2.
Frame the distinction clearly:
substep.template:
In the domain model we identified domain events like {{example_domain_events}}. Now let's look at system-level events — things that trigger handlers outside the domain layer. For example: user registered triggers a welcome email, order placed triggers an inventory check.
What system-level events does this project need?
substep.action: Wait for response.
Collect a trigger/handler/side-effect table:
Ask about async vs sync classification:
substep.template: For each of these handlers, should it run synchronously (inline) or asynchronously (queued)?
substep.action: Wait for response.

action.condition: LANG is php and FRAMEWORK is symfony

substep.action: After base event questions, ask Symfony Messenger-specific questions from php-symfony/discovery.md section 11a: - What Messenger transports are configured? (transport name, DSN type, purpose) - How are message types routed to transports? - What retry strategy is configured for failed messages? - How are permanently failed messages handled? (dead-letter queue, logging, manual review)

substep.title: Operational Concerns
substep.action: Ask about operational concerns that affect the system as a whole. Cover four topics sequentially, one at a time:

q.template: Does this project need API documentation? If so, what format — OpenAPI/Swagger spec, auto-generated docs, or something else?
Does the application need health check or readiness endpoints? If so, what should they verify — database connectivity, external service availability, queue status?
Are there any scheduled tasks or cron jobs the application needs to run? For example: cleanup jobs, report generation, cache warming, data syncs.
What external services does this project integrate with? For example: payment gateways, email providers, SMS services, third-party APIs, cloud storage.

substep.rule: Ask ONE question, wait for answer, then ask the next.

output.file: Four sections: Frontend Presence, Auth Flows, Event Listeners, Operational Concerns. Present summary to user and await confirmation before saving.

step.rule: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist before writing output files.
Present a complete summary of all four sections to the user and ask for confirmation before saving to docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md.

step.title: Test Environment
step.action: Read _base/discovery.md section 14 to inform conversational question flow. Do NOT dump template content to the user — use it as reference for natural follow-up questions.

substep.title: Base Test Environment
substep.action: Ask _base/discovery.md section 14 questions conversationally, one at a time. Cover the six base topics, plus a seventh seed-default-admin topic GATED on auth flows (ask it only when Step 6.2 captured at least one auth flow):

q.template: What is the base URL for the test/development environment? (e.g., http://localhost:8080, https://dev.example.com)
What test user credentials are available? For example: admin username/password, regular user credentials, API tokens.
What is the test database name? Is it a separate database from development?
What command loads test fixtures or seeds the database? (e.g., `php bin/console doctrine:fixtures:load`, `npm run seed`, custom script)
Is Playwright installed and available for E2E testing? - Installed and configured - Not installed — needs Gate 0 setup - Not applicable (API-only, no browser tests needed)
What CI/CD environment does this project use? (e.g., GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, none)

q.condition: Step 6.2 AUTH_FLOWS is non-empty (at least one auth flow mapped)
q.template: Should the app seed a default admin user on first run (the dev environment)? If yes, which environment-variable NAMES hold the admin identifier and the admin password? Provide the NAMES only — for example `APP_ADMIN_EMAIL` and `APP_ADMIN_PASSWORD` — never the secret values themselves.
q.rule: GATED: ask this ONLY when Step 6.2 captured one or more auth flows (AUTH_FLOWS non-empty). If AUTH_FLOWS is empty there is no User entity to seed — SKIP this question entirely and leave seed_default_admin unset.
Capture environment-variable NAMES only (admin_identifier_env, admin_password_env). NEVER record, repeat, or inline the secret values — values live only in the environment.

substep.rule: Ask ONE question, wait for answer, then ask the next. Do not batch.

substep.title: Symfony Test Environment
substep.condition: LANG is php and FRAMEWORK is symfony
substep.action: Ask php-symfony/discovery.md section 14a questions conversationally. These extend the base test environment with Symfony-specific details.

q.template: What PHPUnit configuration is in use? Let me check for `phpunit.xml.dist` or `phpunit.xml` — do you have any custom configuration beyond the defaults?
What database strategy is used for tests? For example: - Separate test database - SQLite in-memory - Transaction rollback per test - Fixtures with database reset
What Symfony test base classes are used? For example: `KernelTestCase`, `WebTestCase`, `ApiTestCase`, or a custom base class?

substep.rule: Ask ONE question, wait for answer, then ask the next. Do not batch.

substep.title: Validation and Save
substep.action: Validate that all required fields from substep 7.1 are non-empty: base URL, run target (docker|local, captured in Step 4.1), test credentials, DB name, fixture command, Playwright status.

check.action: Ask a targeted follow-up for the missing field:
check.template: The **{{field_name}}** is still missing. Could you provide this? It's needed to ensure generated tests can run against the real environment.
check.action: Wait for response and update that field.

check.condition: seed_default_admin == yes AND (admin_identifier_env is blank OR admin_password_env is blank)
check.action: Run a targeted D-14-style follow-up BEFORE saving — one per missing NAME:
check.template: You chose to seed a default admin, but the environment-variable NAME for the **{{missing_admin_env_field}}** is still missing. Which env-var NAME holds it? Provide the NAME only (e.g. `APP_ADMIN_EMAIL`) — never the secret value.
check.action: Wait for response and update that field. Do NOT save until BOTH admin_identifier_env and admin_password_env are non-empty NAMES.

substep.action: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist (mkdir -p).
Present summary to user before saving:

When writing test-environment.md, append a `## Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin` block recording exactly: `seed_default_admin: yes`, `admin_identifier_env: {{admin_identifier_env}}`, `admin_password_env: {{admin_password_env}}`. Record env-var NAMES only — NEVER the secret values. Generation Step 3.19a reads this block directly.
On confirmation, write docs/architecture/test-environment.md.
substep.rule: Do NOT save test-environment.md until user confirms the summary.

The `## Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin` block, when present, records env-var NAMES (admin_identifier_env, admin_password_env) only — never secret values (GM-05). The block is omitted entirely when seed_default_admin is not yes.

D-14 gate: test environment doc must contain base URL, test user credentials, DB name, fixture command, AND Run Target (docker|local) — all non-empty. When Run Target = docker with compose-hosted services, the doc must ALSO contain a non-empty Published Ports mapping (omitted entirely — not "N/A" — for local or external-services projects).
D-15 gate: Playwright status must explicitly state whether Playwright is installed, needs Gate 0 setup, or is not applicable.

output.file: Test environment configuration: base URL, run target (docker|local; plus DB/services location compose|external and published host:container ports when docker with compose services), test credentials, DB name, fixture command, Playwright status, CI/CD environment. Plus Symfony-specific sections when applicable.

step.rule: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist before writing output files.
Present the complete test environment summary to the user and ask for confirmation before saving to docs/architecture/test-environment.md.

step.title: Inventory Generation
step.action: Compile counts from all prior discovery steps:

counts.count: Number of aggregate roots across all BCs
Number of auth flows mapped (Register, Login, Logout, Token Refresh, Password Reset)
Number of operational concerns (API docs, health checks, scheduled tasks, external integrations)
Number of architecture decision records generated
Number of test environment infrastructure items (test framework setup, fixture seeding, CI pipeline, Playwright installation). When Step 7 collected zero items, infrastructure = 0.

step.action: Present the inventory summary table:
step.template: | Category | Count | |----------|-------| | Bounded contexts | {{bc_count}} | | Aggregates | {{aggregate_count}} | | API endpoints | {{endpoint_count}} | | Auth flows | {{auth_flow_count}} | | Event listeners | {{listener_count}} | | Cross-cutting items | {{crosscutting_count}} | | Architecture decisions | {{decision_count}} | | **Estimated total goals** | **{{estimated_goal_count}}** |
step.action: Calculate the estimated goal count using the formula: endpoints + auth_flows + listeners + cross_cutting + decisions + (aggregates * 2) + infrastructure

check.action: Display warning: "This inventory produces over 100 goals. Consider reducing MVP scope or splitting into phases before proceeding."

step.action: Present the confirmation gate:
step.template:
The discovery phase identified **{{estimated_goal_count}}** goals. Do you want to proceed with goal generation, or would you like to adjust the scope first?
Reply **proceed** to continue, or **adjust** to revisit earlier steps.
step.action: Wait for user response before continuing.

branch.condition: user confirms (proceed, yes, confirm, looks good)
branch.action: Acknowledge confirmation and continue to the next step.

branch.condition: user requests adjustment (adjust, no, change, change scope)
branch.action: Print guidance on which steps to revisit:
branch.template:
No problem — here are the steps you can revisit to adjust the scope:
- **Step 2** — Change MVP scope or product brief - **Step 3** — Modify bounded contexts, aggregates, or domain events - **Step 5** — Adjust API endpoints - **Step 6** — Modify cross-cutting concerns, auth flows, or event listeners
Tell me which step you'd like to revisit, and we'll pick up from there. When you're done adjusting, come back and I'll regenerate the inventory.
branch.action: Wait for user to revisit the chosen step. After adjustments, re-run inventory counts and re-present the confirmation gate.

branch.template: I didn't catch that — please reply **proceed** to continue with goal generation, or **adjust** to revisit earlier steps.

step.rule: Do NOT proceed to Step 9 without explicit user confirmation of the inventory. The user must reply "proceed", "confirm", "yes", or equivalent before goal generation can begin. (D-17)

step.title: Context Checkpoint & Handoff
step.action: Verify all expected discovery output files exist on disk. Run existence checks (test -f / test -d) for each file:
step.rule: For context-map.md: skip this check when is_single_bc is true. (D-27)
For adrs/ directory: verify the directory exists AND contains at least one .md file.

check.action: Report each missing file with its source step:
check.template: Missing: {{filename}} — this was expected from {{source_step}} but was not found on disk. You may need to re-run the relevant step or create it manually.
check.action: Do NOT print the handoff message. Wait for the user to address the gaps before proceeding.

branch.action: Run lightweight consistency spot-checks (hints, not blockers):

spot-checks.check: Every BC name in bounded-contexts.md appears in domain-model.md
Every BC with actions in api-endpoints.md exists in bounded-contexts.md

branch.action: If mismatches found, print a warning but do NOT block the handoff:
branch.template: Note: Found some inconsistencies across discovery files (these are warnings, not blockers — the generator will do full validation): {{list_of_mismatches}}
Discovery complete. Run /tmux:task-plan-generate to start goal generation.
branch.action: Skill ends here. Do NOT invoke /tmux:task-plan-generate or any code generation. (D-18)

step.rule: The handoff message MUST be the last output. After printing it, the skill does nothing — no further prompts, no follow-up questions, no auto-invocation of the generator.

execution-rules.rule: All output is documents (product brief, bounded contexts, domain model, etc.) — never generate or modify source code
Language and framework detection requires explicit user confirmation before proceeding to the next step
Product brief requires all 5 sections non-empty (D-04) and user confirmation before saving
All user interaction via text output and wait — never use AskUserQuestion tool
Template content (discovery.md, overlay templates) used as conversational reference — never dumped verbatim to the user
Create docs/architecture/ directory before writing any output files — never assume it exists
User must explicitly confirm inventory before proceeding to context checkpoint (D-17)

llm.critical-rules: * Discovery produces DOCUMENTS ONLY — never generate or modify source code * Language detection requires explicit user confirmation before proceeding * Product brief requires all 5 sections non-empty (D-04) and user confirmation before saving * All user interaction via text output + wait — never use AskUserQuestion * Conversational tone — present findings and invite refinement, not rigid Q&A * Subsequent tasks append Steps 3+ above the closing comment in <flow> * User must explicitly confirm inventory before proceeding to context checkpoint (D-17)
